articles.title: Gregory Bovino’s Final Days: Harsh Words and Few Regrets
articles.summary: He was the face of the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown. But as he begins a retirement that was not entirely voluntary, the Border Patrol leader says he did not go far enough.

articles.title: Stephen Miller Asks Why Texas Pays to Teach Undocumented Children
articles.summary: Citing gridlock in Washington, President Trump’s top immigration adviser encouraged Texas lawmakers to lead on conservative priorities.

articles.title: Israel Plans to Control Large Parts of Southern Lebanon, Defense Minister Says
articles.summary: Israel Katz, the Israeli defense minister, suggested that Israeli troops might remain in parts of Lebanon even after the fighting there winds down.

articles.title: Will President Trump act on his threat to take Cuba?
articles.summary: New Yorker writer Jon Lee Anderson describes conditions in Cuba, why it's vulnerable now — and what regime change would mean — considering the Castro family's entrenchment in the Cuban government.

articles.title: David A. Ross Sought Epstein’s Help to Build Art Museums. Now He’s Facing the Fallout.
articles.summary: He mastered the world of the “Epstein Class” to build great museums. Now he’s confronting the cost.

articles.title: Saudi Leader Is Said to Push Trump to Continue Iran War in Recent Calls
articles.summary: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman sees a “historic opportunity” to remake the region, according to people briefed by U.S. officials on the conversations.

articles.title: Before running for Congress, Bobby Pulido was a Tejano music icon
articles.summary: Pulido has been a mainstay of Tejano music —a genre blending traditional regional Mexican elements with country, pop and conjunto influences — for more than three decades.

articles.title: 34 Former Military Members Were Put on Deportation Track in the Past Year
articles.summary: The Trump administration has ramped up enforcement against immigrant service members and their families in its wider crackdown.

articles.title: Wicked Stepmother No Longer, a Female Pharoah Gets a Reputational Makeover
articles.summary: A reassessment of damaged 3,500-year-old statuary adds to evidence that Queen Hatshepsut wasn’t the villain that scholars long took her to be.

articles.title: Senate confirms Sen. Mullin as DHS secretary. And, Iran denies U.S. talks to end war
articles.summary: The Senate has confirmed Markwayne Mullin as the next Department of Homeland Security secretary. And, Iran has denied that it's in talks with the U.S. to end the war, which is now in its fourth week.

articles.title: Pakistan is poised to host the U.S. and Iran for talks to end the war
articles.summary: The Pakistani prime minister said his country stands ready to host negotiations toward a settlement as the war with Iran nears the one-month mark.

articles.title: Kim vows to 'irreversibly' cement North Korea's nuclear status
articles.summary: In his speech, Kim expressed pride in the country's rapid expansion of nuclear weapons and missiles in recent years, calling it the "right" choice.

articles.title: Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies
articles.summary: Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.

articles.title: College Graduates Are Facing the Grimmest Job Market in Years
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence could reshape work, but for now a low-hire, low-fire labor market is the main impediment for young people seeking employment.

articles.title: Denmark holds early elections spurred by Trump's threats to take Greenland
articles.summary: Denmark's prime minister called early parliamentary elections after gaining a popularity boost from standing up to President Trump over his threat to seize Greenland.

articles.title: Airstrikes may have destroyed Iran's last F-14s, ending a long, strange saga
articles.summary: The F-14 was made famous in Top Gun. The U.S. sold the planes to Iran in the 1970s, only for the two countries to become enemies. Iran kept its F-14s flying for decades in the face of U.S. sanctions.

articles.title: How weeks of war have reshaped Iran's negotiating position
articles.summary: How has Iran's negotiating position changed after weeks of war? NPR's A Martinez talks to Mohammad Ali Shabani, editor of the London-based news site Amwaj.media.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
